channel.title: Internode Content
channel.description: Root-level answers, use cases, and updates from Internode.

item.title: AI knowledge management for consultants: keep what you learn
item.description: A practical guide to AI knowledge management for consultants: how to capture and retrieve what you learn across client engagements without a database.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI knowledge management for government: memory that survives turnover
item.description: How public agencies use AI knowledge management to preserve institutional memory across elected turnover, FOIA requests, and multi-year programs.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: AI meeting notes for schools: board, staff, and IEP conversations
item.description: How schools use AI meeting notes to capture decisions from board meetings, staff sessions, and IEP conversations, with FERPA handled honestly.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: AI meeting prep for executive assistants: the brief they'll read
item.description: How executive assistants use AI meeting prep to draft briefs executives will actually read, grounded in past conversations and commitments.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: AI phone call transcription for small business: calls to knowledge
item.description: How small businesses use AI phone call transcription to capture pricing, orders, commitments, and follow-ups from calls without typing notes or keeping a CRM.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI PM agent: what it actually is and what to demand from one
item.description: An AI PM agent captures, organizes, and changes project state from conversations and chat. Here is what separates a real one from a chat box.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: An AI PM that captures tasks from meetings
item.description: An AI PM that captures tasks from meetings should produce real tasks linked to the moment they were decided, not bullet lists inside a transcript.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Business case template for a knowledge management tool
item.description: A copy-paste business case template for a knowledge management tool: problem, cost of inaction, options, ROI, rollout plan, and success metrics.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to calculate the ROI of an AI knowledge tool
item.description: A step-by-step ROI calculator for an AI knowledge tool, with worked examples for a 20-person team, so you can show a manager a real number.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to propose new software to your manager
item.description: A playbook for proposing new software internally: framing the problem, pre-empting procurement objections, piloting, quantifying pain, and timing.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to stop typing tasks from meetings
item.description: A practical guide to ending retyping meeting action items into Linear, Jira, or Asana: four changes that keep the plan current without a scribe.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to synthesize knowledge across client meetings
item.description: A step-by-step guide to synthesizing knowledge across many client meetings using structured capture and cross-meeting topic clustering, not memory.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Internode vs Asana AI Studio: plans from what your team decided
item.description: Internode vs Asana AI Studio on AI work plans: plans built from meeting decisions, cross-source grounding, and two-way sync with Linear or Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Asana AI: which AI task manager should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Asana AI on an AI PM agent: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, bulk mutations, and cross-meeting deduplication.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs ChatGPT for documents: drafts from your team's memory
item.description: Internode vs ChatGPT for documents: drafting from your team's decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs ClickUp AI: plans built from your team's decisions
item.description: Internode vs ClickUp AI on AI work plans: building plans from meeting decisions, tracing sections to conversations, two-way sync with Linear or Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Coda AI: living documents updated from the real world
item.description: Internode vs Coda AI on living documents: auto-updating from meetings, email, and chat, traceable updates, and a proposal flow instead of silent edits.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Confluence AI: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Confluence AI on an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and memory-aware drafting.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Fathom: the meeting brief before you walk in
item.description: Internode vs Fathom on meeting prep drafting: grounding in decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and a real research loop.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Fellow: drafts from your team's decision history
item.description: Internode vs Fellow on AI document drafting: team-wide decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Fireflies AI: meeting briefs from your team's memory
item.description: Internode vs Fireflies AI on meeting prep drafting: decision history grounding, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and a research loop.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Gemini for documents: grounded in your team's memory
item.description: Internode vs Gemini for documents: drafting from the team's decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Glean: drafts from your real decisions
item.description: Internode vs Glean on AI document drafting: the team's decision history, section-level citations, a research loop, and auto-updating drafts.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Granola Prep: the meeting brief you'll actually read
item.description: Internode vs Granola Prep on meeting prep drafting: decision history grounding, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and a research loop.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Granola: which meeting intelligence tool wins?
item.description: Internode vs Granola on AI meeting intelligence: phone calls, email threads, decision rationale, two-way Linear and Jira sync, and org-wide search.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Guru: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Guru on an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and organizational memory.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Jira: which AI PM agent should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Jira on an AI PM agent: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, compound proposals, and two-way sync back into Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Letta: which memory layer should your AI agent use?
item.description: Internode vs Letta on AI agent memory: team-scoped structured memory, a decision-to-source trail, real conversation ingestion, and two-way tool sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Linear: which AI PM agent should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Linear on an AI PM agent: capture from conversations, decision-to-task provenance, bulk mutations, and two-way sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Mem0: which memory layer should your AI agent use?
item.description: Internode vs Mem0 on AI agent memory: team-scoped structured memory, a decision-to-source trail, real conversation ingestion, and two-way tool sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Microsoft Copilot: drafts from your team's decisions
item.description: Internode vs Microsoft Copilot for documents: drafting from your team's decision history, section-level citations, and a research loop over your memory.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Microsoft Syntex: AI drafts grounded in your policies
item.description: Internode vs Microsoft Syntex: grounding policy documents in both company policy and your team's live decisions, with per-paragraph citations.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Notion AI: drafts from your team's memory
item.description: Internode vs Notion AI for documents: drafting from the team's decision history, section-level citations, and auto-updating when decisions change.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Notion AI: which AI should manage your team's knowledge?
item.description: Internode vs Notion AI on AI knowledge management: conversations as input, structured records, decision-to-source trail, and memory-aware drafting.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Notion as a wiki: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Notion as a wiki on an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, a decision-to-source trail, and zero page-writing.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Otter: meeting briefs from your team's knowledge
item.description: Internode vs Otter on meeting prep drafting: grounding in decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and a real research loop.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Otter: which AI meeting intelligence tool should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Otter on AI meeting intelligence: phone calls, email threads, structured tasks, decision rationale, and two-way Linear and Jira sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Read AI: which meeting intelligence tool wins?
item.description: Internode vs Read AI on AI meeting intelligence: phone calls, email threads, structured tasks, decision rationale, and two-way Linear and Jira sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Slab: which AI knowledge base should you use?
item.description: Internode vs Slab on an AI knowledge base: conversations as input, structured records, the decision-to-source trail, and memory-aware drafting.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs tldv: the meeting brief your team will actually use
item.description: Internode vs tldv on meeting prep drafting: grounding in decision history, cross-meeting context, per-section citations, and a real research loop.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode vs Zep: which memory layer should your AI agent use?
item.description: Internode vs Zep on AI agent memory: team-scoped structured memory, a decision-to-source trail, real conversation ingestion, and two-way tool sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Memory-aware drafting: docs that know what your team decided
item.description: Memory-aware drafting is document generation grounded in organizational memory: every paragraph traces to a decision, meeting, or policy you produced.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: The AI knowledge base that builds itself
item.description: Most AI knowledge bases are wikis with a chat box. One that builds itself never needs updating because it learns from the conversations already happening.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The AI-native alternative to Notion: a self-writing knowledge system
item.description: Notion is a workspace-as-database that you build. An AI-native alternative starts from conversations and produces structured knowledge without typing pages.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The alternative to a CRM for consulting knowledge
item.description: CRMs track contacts and deals. Consultants need a system for what they learned from conversations and patterns across engagements. Here is that system.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: The best AI knowledge management tools in 2026
item.description: A ranked look at the AI knowledge management tools that matter in 2026, ordered by how much of the work the tool still asks you to do yourself.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The best AI task manager in 2026
item.description: A ranked comparison of the best AI task managers in 2026 on what keeps a plan current: conversation capture, provenance, bulk edits, and two-way sync.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: The best second brain app 2026: an honest ranking
item.description: An honest 2026 ranking of second brain apps for people who tried Notion, Obsidian, Roam, and Logseq. Internode ranks first because it builds itself.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: The cost of lost team knowledge, per employee, per year
item.description: A per-employee dollar figure for lost team knowledge, built from IDC, McKinsey, Panopto, and Gartner research on search time, rework, and onboarding.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: What is decision memory?
item.description: Decision memory is the slice of team knowledge that holds final decisions, their rationale, and how they change over time. Here is where it fits.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What is organizational memory?
item.description: Organizational memory is the structured, searchable record of what your team has decided, agreed, and committed to across meetings, calls, and chat.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: AI knowledge management tools for government and public orgs
item.description: A guide to AI tools that help government agencies, schools, healthcare organizations, and nonprofits manage institutional knowledge and track outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: AI meeting notes versus organizational memory
item.description: A clear comparison of AI meeting note tools and organizational memory systems, what each does well, and where the gap lies for engineering teams.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: AI-first vs AI-added: why bolting AI onto Notion is not enough
item.description: AI-added tools bolt intelligence onto manual workflows. AI-first tools remove the manual layer. That difference determines whether your system decays.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: Building a business case for organizational intelligence
item.description: A framework for building a business case for knowledge management tools, with ROI calculations, audience-specific framing, and answers to common objections.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: From conversations to knowledge: what professionals actually need
item.description: Professionals across meetings, client calls, and research need a system that turns conversational knowledge into searchable intelligence, automatically.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How executive assistants stop being the only one who remembers
item.description: Executive assistants carry the memory for their executives. Here is how to move that knowledge into a system that survives your next vacation.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How healthcare teams keep care coordination decisions organized
item.description: How healthcare organizations track care coordination decisions across shifts, staff changes, and department meetings to maintain continuity.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How schools preserve institutional knowledge when staff leave
item.description: Practical strategies for schools and districts to preserve institutional knowledge when experienced staff transfer or retire.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: How small businesses stop losing information from phone calls
item.description: Practical steps for small businesses to stop losing customer details, pricing agreements, and commitments from phone conversations.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How solving your team's knowledge problem advances your career
item.description: Being the person who fixes a systemic knowledge problem at work is a career-defining contribution. Here is why it matters and how to make it visible.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to build a briefing system that does not depend on your memory
item.description: Most EA briefing systems rely on personal memory. Here is how to build one that generates context automatically from past meetings and conversations.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to capture decisions from meetings without writing everything down
item.description: A practical guide to capturing what was decided, assigned, and discussed in meetings using transcription instead of manual note-taking.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to connect meeting decisions to project tasks
item.description: How to close the gap between what your team agrees to in meetings and what actually shows up in Linear or Jira, with traceable links between decisions and work.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How to organize customer and supplier commitments without a CRM
item.description: How small businesses can track customer orders, supplier agreements, and verbal commitments without buying or learning a CRM.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to propose a knowledge tool when you have no budget authority
item.description: A step-by-step guide for employees who want to propose a knowledge management tool to their manager when they do not control the budget.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to tell if your team has a knowledge management problem
item.description: Seven warning signs that your team is losing knowledge from meetings, conversations, and staff transitions, and what to do when you spot the pattern.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: How to track decisions from board meetings and committee sessions
item.description: A practical approach to tracking decisions from board meetings and committee sessions so they stay findable and usable long after the meeting ends.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: How to turn phone calls into searchable business knowledge
item.description: A practical guide to transcribing business phone calls and turning them into organized, searchable records your whole team can access.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Knowledge management for people who gave up on knowledge management
item.description: If you have tried and abandoned Notion, Obsidian, or Roam, the problem was not you. Here is what a zero-maintenance alternative looks like.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: The hidden cost of scattered knowledge at work
item.description: Scattered knowledge costs teams thousands of hours per year in repeated work, slow onboarding, and forgotten follow-ups. Here is how to quantify the problem.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: The knowledge system that builds itself
item.description: A knowledge system that organizes itself from your conversations, meetings, and documents. No filing, no tagging, no maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What changes when your team actually remembers what was decided
item.description: When what your team discussed and agreed on is preserved and searchable, meetings get shorter, onboarding gets faster, and follow-through improves.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: What happens to your office when the EA leaves
item.description: When an executive assistant leaves, the organizational knowledge they carry disappears with them. Here is what that costs and how to prevent it.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: What is institutional knowledge and why teams lose it
item.description: A clear definition of institutional knowledge, why organizations lose it when staff leave, and what to do about it.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What is organizational memory for AI agents?
item.description: A practical definition of organizational memory for AI agents: what it contains, how it differs from raw search, and why it improves retrieval.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: What to look for in an AI knowledge management tool
item.description: A practical checklist for evaluating AI knowledge management tools, written for engineering leads and PMs who need to separate real capabilities from demo-ware.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why AI agents need decision memory
item.description: Why retrieval alone is not enough for AI agents and how decision memory improves answer quality, speed, and trust.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why note-taking apps fail knowledge workers
item.description: Note-taking apps assume your knowledge comes from reading and typing. For professionals whose knowledge comes from conversations, the model is wrong.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why small businesses forget what was decided and how to fix it
item.description: Why small businesses lose track of their own agreements and practical steps to fix the problem without adding complexity.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Why your best work knowledge comes from conversations, not documents
item.description: The most valuable professional knowledge lives in conversations, not documents. Conversations capture the reasoning and context that matter later.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Why your meeting prep takes hours and how to cut it in half
item.description: Executive assistants spend 10 to 12 hours a week on meeting prep and follow-ups. Here is why it takes so long and how to reduce it dramatically.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why your second brain keeps failing
item.description: Second brain systems fail because they turn you into a librarian of your own knowledge. The problem is the paradigm, not the tool.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Why your team keeps re-discussing the same decisions
item.description: Teams re-discuss the same decisions because what was agreed, and why, is not recorded anywhere findable. Here is why it happens and what to do about it.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Executive assistants tracking decisions across 50 meetings a week
item.description: How an EA supporting three executives uses a persistent memory layer to track decisions, commitments, and follow-ups across 50+ meetings per week.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Healthcare team tracking decisions across shifts and staff changes
item.description: How a healthcare organization tracks care coordination decisions across shifts, department meetings, and staff transitions.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: New executive assistant onboarding without predecessor documentation
item.description: How a new EA uses a persistent knowledge base to onboard in weeks instead of months when no predecessor documentation exists.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: School district preserving knowledge across staff transitions
item.description: How a school district preserves institutional knowledge when principals transfer, teachers retire, and coordinators change roles.
item.dc:creator: Istvan Lorincz

item.title: Small business capturing decisions from phone calls automatically
item.description: How a small reselling business captures customer and supplier details from phone calls using transcription and AI, without manual data entry.
item.dc:creator: Sean Shadmand

item.title: Turning calls and meetings into structured knowledge for any team
item.description: How teams of any size turn phone calls, Zoom meetings, and Slack conversations into structured, searchable knowledge they can reuse.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Use case: product and engineering alignment
item.description: How persistent knowledge tracking keeps product and engineering teams aligned without repeating debates about scope, priorities, and tradeoffs.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: How Internode works with phone calls and meeting recordings
item.description: How Internode processes phone transcripts, meeting recordings from Zoom and Google Meet, Slack, and typed notes into structured team knowledge.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode content hub launch
item.description: Why Internode now publishes root-level answer pages on content.internode.ai, designed for both human readers and AI systems.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi

item.title: Internode integrations with Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, and email
item.description: How Internode connects to Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, email, phone transcripts, and task trackers like Linear and Jira.
item.dc:creator: Balazs Ketyi
